nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note1

parameters.content:
## Send an error message:
1. If query param was incorrect, return the instruction. AI Agent should pick up on this and adapt the query on the next iteration.
2. If the query is OK and an error was during the HTTP Request, then send back the original error message.

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note2

parameters.content:
## Post-processing of the HTML page:
1. Keep only <BODY> content
2. Remove inline <SCRIPT> tag entirely, as well as: NOSCRIPT, IFRAME, OBJECT, EMBED, VIDEO, AUDIO, SVG, and HTML comments.
3. In case query parameter method=simplified, replace all page URLs (a href) and IMG (src) with NOURL / NOIMG - this may save up to 20% of the page length
4. Convert the remaining HTML to Markdown. This step further reduces the length of the page: long HTML tags and styles are eliminated, but the markdown syntax keeps some page structure. This gives much better results compared to just a blank text.
5. Finally, check the page length. If it's too long, send an "ERROR: PAGE CONTENT TOO LONG" instead of the actual page. Of course, you could split the page content in chunks, but sometimes long pages just don't have a needed content, so it makes little sense to burn tokens on them.

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: HTTP_Request_Tool

parameters.name: HTTP_Request_Tool
parameters.description: Call this tool to fetch a webpage content. The input should be a stringified HTTP query parameter like this: "?url=VALIDURL&method=SELECTEDMETHOD". "url" parameter should contain the valid URL string. "method" key can be either "full" or "simplified". method=full will fetch the whole webpage content in the Markdown format, including page links and image links. method=simplified will return the Markdown content of the page but remove urls and image links from the page content for simplicity. Before calling this tool, think strategically which "method" to call. Best of all to use method=simplified. However, if you anticipate that the page request is not final or if you need to extract links from the page, pick method=full.

nodes.notes: This toolWorkflow node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

"method" key can be either "full" or "simplified". method=full will fetch the whole webpage content in the Markdown format, including page links and image links. method=simplified will return the Markdown content of the page but remove urls and image links from the page content for simplicity. Before calling this tool, think strategically which "method" to call. Best of all to use method=simplified. However, if you anticipate that the page request is not final or if you need to extract links from the page, pick method=full.

values.name: HTML
values.stringValue: ={{ ($json.HTML || "HTML BODY CONTENT FOR THIS SEARCH RESULT IS NOT AVAILABLE").replace(/<script[^>]*>([\s\S]*?)<\/script>|<style[^>]*>([\s\S]*?)<\/style>|<noscript[^>]*>([\s\S]*?)<\/noscript>|<!--[\s\S]*?-->|<iframe[^>]*>([\s\S]*?)<\/iframe>|<object[^>]*>([\s\S]*?)<\/object>|<embed[^>]*>([\s\S]*?)<\/embed>|<video[^>]*>([\s\S]*?)<\/video>|<audio[^>]*>([\s\S]*?)<\/audio>|<svg[^>]*>([\s\S]*?)<\/svg>/ig, '')}}

parameters.content: ### Convert the query string into JSON, apply the limit for a page length

## Send an error message:
1. If query param was incorrect, return the instruction. AI Agent should pick up on this and adapt the query on the next iteration.
2. If the query is OK and an error was during the HTTP Request, then send back the original error message.

## Post-processing of the HTML page:
1. Keep only <BODY> content
2. Remove inline <SCRIPT> tag entirely, as well as: NOSCRIPT, IFRAME, OBJECT, EMBED, VIDEO, AUDIO, SVG, and HTML comments.
3. In case query parameter method=simplified, replace all page URLs (a href) and IMG (src) with NOURL / NOIMG - this may save up to 20% of the page length
4. Convert the remaining HTML to Markdown. This step further reduces the length of the page: long HTML tags and styles are eliminated, but the markdown syntax keeps some page structure. This gives much better results compared to just a blank text.
5. Finally, check the page length. If it's too long, send an "ERROR: PAGE CONTENT TOO LONG" instead of the actual page. Of course, you could split the page content in chunks, but sometimes long pages just don't have a needed content, so it makes little sense to burn tokens on them.

## Example ReAct AI Agent
1. Agent Prompt is default
2. Check the description of the HTTP_Request_Tool, it guides the agent to provide a query string with several parameters instead of a JSON object

Only a simple string value is accepted. JSON object as an action_input is NOT supported!" : JSON.stringify($json.error) }}

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: open Ai  A P I details
configurationSteps: Verify the settings of the trigger node "Execute Workflow Trigger".
Open the "On new manual Chat Message" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "OpenAI Chat Model" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "HTTP Request" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Is error?" node (n8n-nodes-base.if) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Simplify?" node (n8n-nodes-base.if) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note1" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note2" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note3" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "ReAct AI Agent" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Convert to Markdown" node (n8n-nodes-base.markdown) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "HTTP_Request_Tool" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Exctract HTML Body".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Stringify error message".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Remove extra tags".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.

Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "CONFIG".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Send Page Content".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
